developer_response.body:
Hi leel33,

Thanks for taking the time to explain what you’re running into. What you’re describing is usually caused by physically stepping forward during swings rather than the game moving your position on its own.

Match Point Tennis is designed to be played stationary, and the game uses your real-world position as the reference point. If you take small steps forward while swinging, those movements add up and can place you closer to walls over time.

A few things that should help:
- In Settings → Movement Orientation, try switching between Headset-Based (recommended for stationary play) and Center-Based to see which feels more stable for your setup.

- If you tend to move while playing, Auto Run works better than Auto Teleport, since it lets you fine-tune your distance from the ball using the thumbstick.

- Keep the Center Marker enabled (it’s on by default). The X on the floor shows your original play position. If you notice it behind you, you’ve physically drifted forward and can step back safely without needing passthrough.

- You can also play seated if staying in one place is more comfortable. The game fully supports seated play and can be a good option if you find yourself naturally walking during swings.

On serving, the game intentionally limits movement so players can’t reposition themselves unfairly before the serve. If you’re not standing near your center point when serving, it can feel restrictive until you step back into position.

We understand space and comfort are important in VR, and we’ve added these tools specifically to help players stay oriented and safe. If you’d like more help dialing this in for your room setup, feel free to reach out and we’ll be happy to help.

reviews.review_title: An Ace that deserves a Smashing review
reviews.review_description:
I've never played tennis IRL, but this VR experience feels quite real to me.
The price is Set to free, so take it for a topspin. Then you might realise getting pro-version has some Advantage, like more courts, more AI-opponents, and private rooms for matches with friends.
When I started playing I felt like I was in No Man's Land, but my Game has improved and I start winning against hard AI.
MatchPoint Tennis is right up my Alley and gets all my Love.
Stuck on loading screen but good game

This game is super fun, great physics and genuinely best gameplay, I really would love a mode against ai or the ball machine to play without Internet connection or start up without Internet connection, so I can play wherever and whenever!

I am still waiting for the game to install but i hope it will be fun i will update soon. I played the game and it was real
y fun. i ,mostly played practice and my score on practice was 94 shots i will update tomorrow.
give me a big fan of tennis put this is amazing cuz you can actually hit the ball
What a great game! Today I jumped up in hope to catch a volley and hit it exactly where I expected. Very cool physics.

The game is great, with tennis physics that feel realistic as a real tennis player. The one critical flaw is that players can move all the way up to the net. This allows anyone to easily smack any ball that comes their way straight down at maximum speed, ending the point with no skill involved. This becomes a problem especially in ranked matches when players with rush the net on every point for the sole purpose of smacking the ball down to auto end play. It ruins the otherwise realistic feel of the game, and makes ranked matches a waste.

We group of friends purchased this app, but after update multi player lobby is not working. App doesnt go ahead after clicking on begin button. Most disappointing with this app from long time after app update. Team Focused how to grab money from avatars, costumes and accessories but not on the game and users satisfaction.

developer_response.body:
Hello Animandava - sorry to hear you were affected by our multiplayer server provider going down. It happened at the worst time for us, and also for you and your friends. We got the issue solved with the provider in under 24 hours, but understand that soured your experience.

Hope to hear from you soon - feel free to reach out to us at community@backyardgamestudio.com and we'd be happy to chat with you.

reviews.review_description:
I truly enjoy playing this game in my free time whenever I can; it's a great workout (with the correct settings) and is fun to play against other too.  The new update adds more flair and appeal to the game as well; however,  private and public lobbies are not working at the moment, and I'm not able to play with my siblings. I also think it's a little ridiculous that you have to be a Pro member to host a Private lobby; I can see how having a Pro account protects you from accessing other features in the game but playing in a private lobby doesn't seem right (especially if you're charging $14+ for a Pro account). 

While I'm thinking about it, for loyal players with Pro accounts, it would be nice to have a discount when purchasing new items/maps (more map options would be nice instead of maps having a 'night mode'). This is truly a fun game, and it's my hope that it can be an enjoyable experience for everyone who plays.

The game has a lot of potential, but the fact that if i'm in my backyard, I can't fully move around without going out of boundaries and it's difficult to reset the boundaries on the midfle of a match.  I'm just gonna waste my money if I'm always outdide thd boundary when clearly I'm not. fix the boundary errors and you have a new customer.

developer_response.body:
Hello MsKeitaSan - just touching base, thanks for sharing your thoughts. 
Are you having issues where the game places you in the wrong position in front of the line, or are you looking to play the game where you move your feet and run around, like in real Tennis?

If the former, please send us a message to community@backyardgamestudio.com - we'd love to look into that for you. If the issue is that the boundaries on your Meta Quest come up if you run through them, that is a Meta Safety feature and by design. 

If I misunderstand what you're saying is the issue, please reach out to us, and we're more than happy to get you sorted out right away. 

Thank you and take care.

Happy to chat more about the new release, but to be honest, we have not touched the physics engine in any way, so if you're experiencing something else, we'd like to help get to the bottom of it with you if you're interested.

Feel free to reach out to us at community@backyardgamestudio.com and we'll hop in a multiplayer session to learn more about what you're describing.

developer_response.body:
Hi Grimaidi,

Thanks for reporting this and sorry for the frustration. You ran into a bug introduced with that update that affected players in certain regions and could cause matches to get stuck in the state you described. Sorry for the delay in responses - we're just getting to the reviews now after a wild 2 week ride of updates.

We were able to identify and resolve the issue within about 24 hours of launch, and it should now be fixed. You should be able to start fresh matches normally without being pulled back into the old one.

Thank you for taking the time to explain what was happening. Reports like this helped us isolate the problem quickly. If anything still feels off, please let us know.

reviews.review_description:

on discord. 




Update: November 2025

The big update has launched.  If there was any doubt this game is clearly the best VR tennis game out. The update is awesome. It's a new era BUY the game you won't be disappointed.  Developers say there's more to come.

developer_response.body:
Thank you, Pova24. We really appreciate the review and, just as importantly, the long-term support you’ve shown since day one.

Your perspective carries a lot of weight, especially coming from someone who has spent real time with the game across multiple updates and has been an active part of the Discord community. The way you encourage people to judge the game on its own merits, explore the different courts and AI opponents, and actually spend time with it before reviewing, means a great deal to us.

We’re glad the November update landed well for you, and we appreciate you sticking with us through the growing pains and improvements along the way. There is indeed more to come, and it’s community members like you who help shape the direction we take.

Thanks again for being part of the Match Point Tennis community.

reviews.review_description:
When I bought the game I loved it and, with the last update, it turned even better. I only have one request for you, a free clay court because nowadays I have to pay to play on clay courts. I'm gonna buy premium for Christmas but I've been playing this game for a year and I would have liked a free clay court. 
In conclusion, the game is the best tennis game I've played with my VR glasses.
Thank you for this game.

developer_response.body:
Hi jabigar,

Thank you for the kind review and for sticking with the game for such a long time. It really means a lot to us to hear from someone who has been playing regularly for a year and is still enjoying the experience.

I also appreciate you sharing your thoughts about the clay courts. Just to clarify, clay courts are already available to everyone through practice mode, and they rotate into multiplayer during the month. The PRO version unlocks permanent access and additional courts, along with other extras, as a way for players who play often to support the ongoing development and server costs behind the game.

Keeping the core experience free is important to us, but PRO exists so that players who spend a lot of time in the game can help keep it sustainable and growing. Updates, servers, new courts, and continued improvements are all made possible by that support.

Thank you again for the thoughtful feedback and for planning to pick up PRO. We’re glad you’re enjoying the game, and we hope you continue to have fun on court.

reviews.review_title: Can no longer get into the game
reviews.review_description: It used to be great but now always glitches and the game no longer loads up.

This is a really fun game. Very realistic. And you get a great workout! You really have to hit the ball as hard as you would in a real game, so you get a great workout. I'm using the free version, which only allows you to compete against easy or medium level players. So far the medium level player is enough for me. If I need to play against someone harder, I may upgrade to the Pro version.  But, all in all, I love this game, and play it just about every day. It's one of my main workout apps. And I've tried other tennis apps, and they're just not as good. Highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Egads - this review is incredible. Thank you very much for your support and we're glad you're enjoying the game - more on the way!

All the grips except Eastern send the ball flying and even in practice it messes up my serves please fix it and even eastern if u don’t hit slow it goes everywhere. New update sucks.

developer_response.body:
Hi HockeyQC_QC44,
Thanks for calling this out. You’re right that there was a grip-related issue in that initial update, and we understand how frustrating that is, especially when it affects serving and basic control.

The problem was identified and fixed within about 48 hours of launch. The timing unfortunately overlapped with a weekend, which slowed our response a bit, but all grips and controller adapters should now be behaving correctly.

If you’re still seeing anything off, please let us know. We appreciate you taking the time to report it, and we hope the game feels better to play now.

reviews.review_description: This by far my favourite game in the library as a parent game!! 🎾

reviews.review_title: Alot of flaws need to be fixed
reviews.review_description: Was good now cant play ranked or public games. It places me in the middle of court and then nothing. Very sad being i played everyday for the last 6 months. Please fix the flaws
New to the Meta Quest 3 and am absolutely loving this game, If I buy the pro version can you have a full match or play a tournament?

Have tried a few tennis apps but this works the best for me, the interface is very intuitive unlike some others, and the mechanics feel consistent and easy to dial in. Maybe not the most feature rich but for a casual player like me it's spot on and of all the tennis apps this is the one I come back to. The AI opponents are Great to play and train with, it would be good to have more of them. The real player interactions I've had have been challenging and fun. Just started to try the ranked matches, getting used to auto run is a bit dizzying but I'm getting used to it. I was happy to buy the pro version, most other tennis games want a subscription which is an instant turn off for me.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind and detailed review. We really appreciate you taking the time to share what’s working well for you.

It’s great to hear that the interface and core mechanics feel intuitive and consistent. That balance of approachability and depth is something we care a lot about, especially for players who want a comfortable, pick-up-and-play experience. Your feedback on the AI opponents is noted as well. Expanding and improving them is something we actively want to do, both for training and long-term variety.

We’re also glad you gave Ranked mode a try. Auto-run can definitely take a bit of adjustment, and it’s good to hear it’s starting to click. Hearing that online matches have been challenging and fun is especially encouraging.

Thank you as well for supporting the game with the PRO version. We made a very deliberate choice to avoid subscriptions, so it means a lot to hear that decision resonated with you.

Hope to see you back on court, and thanks again for playing.

reviews.review_title: Wish an update with inside-out forehand
reviews.review_description: So I (and I guess 99% of players) choose auto movement mode, which is awesome. The only thing I wish the game had is somehow implementing strategic movement to perform inside-out forehand, especially when we have time to move against high lobs. The current movement always force us to use backhands in these situations. Other than this, the game is perfect.
My game freezes after the first serve in single player mode. I don't get a new ball for the next serve.

developer_response.body:
Hi ZAC.ZAC,

Thanks for the balanced review and for clearly describing what you ran into. After the November update, there was a bug that caused the single player mode to freeze after the first serve for some players, which matches what you experienced.

We were able to identify and fix that issue within about 24 hours of the update, and single player matches should now progress normally again.

If you have a chance to jump back in, we’d be interested to hear how it feels now. Your report helped us get to the root of the problem more quickly, and we appreciate you taking the time to share it.

reviews.review_description: Kept loosing, went try-hard mode, launches controller at tv…….. it was on easy :’(

developer_response.body:
Hi M0uKXo_O,

That’s an unfortunate way to learn how immersive VR can be. We’re sorry to hear about the TV, and we hope nothing else was damaged.

A quick reminder for anyone reading: always use the wrist straps and keep a clear play area, especially when matches get intense. Even on Easy, tennis can still get competitive.

Thanks for giving the game a try, and we hope your next session is a bit less costly.

reviews.review_title: Best legit one out there yet
reviews.review_description: I’ve tried them all but this one has the most realistic motions. Although I wish they did a bit better with the slices, there’s not much else to complain about. Although it’s free, the game is so good I’m considering upgrading it to premium, also mostly because they don’t shove it down your throat to upgrade like all the other games do.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful review, Jonnieboychoi. We really appreciate you taking the time to spell out what’s working well and where there’s still room to improve.

Hearing that the motion and overall feel stand out, especially compared to other tennis games, means a lot to us. Your note about slices is fair and helpful, and it’s something we continue to examine as we review the physics and shot variety.

We also appreciate the callout on how we handle upgrades. Keeping the core experience free and avoiding pressure to upgrade was a deliberate choice, so it’s good to hear that approach comes across.

Thanks again for playing and for sharing such balanced feedback.

reviews.review_description: Best VR tennis game but I don't understand Something,everybody plays the easy mode,most of the people ,so why on rank you don't alow for easy mechanics ,only amateur(normal) and hard,when most of the people 90% plays on easy,let me chose how I want to play ,easy feel best that's why everybody is playing like this ,so let  me in tournaments and ranked match to play on easy ,don't force only normal and hard ,this is really non sense...I'm disappointed here ,but it's still the best tennis game ,so I wait maybe they put also the easy mechanics on ranked also , everyone play on easy because the game feel best that way ,so let us chose how to play in ranked also.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful and detailed feedback: and for still calling it the best VR tennis game, even with this frustration.

You’re absolutely right that Easy mechanics feel great for a lot of players. The reason Ranked currently uses Amateur and Pro physics is to keep competitive matches consistent and fair across players, but we understand why being locked out of Ranked on your preferred mechanics feels limiting. This is feedback we’ve seen from others as well and it’s something we continue to discuss internally.

Since this review, we’ve released a major update that was a year in the making that adds a full progression system with XP earned across all modes, plus daily and weekly challenges that let you progress and unlock cosmetics regardless of how you prefer to play. If part of what you were looking for was more ways to engage and progress outside Ranked, that experience has grown a lot.

We appreciate you sticking with us, and we’d love for you to jump back in and see how the game feels now.

reviews.review_description: I can't join a match I load into the game and it's normal but I can't load into any matches.
1 second im a dwarf the next im the eiffel tower please fix it

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful review and the 4 out of 5 stars, we really appreciate it. Your feedback is clear and helpful, and it’s great to see you enjoying the game while also pointing out something we can improve.

As mentioned, Match Point Tennis uses an automatic height calibration system with realistic human ranges to keep gameplay fair. Sudden changes in height are often tied to boundary calibration or being right on the edge of the tolerance range, which can cause the system to snap to a default value.

We’d be happy to dig into this with you directly. If you’re up for it, please reach out to us at community@backyardgamestudio.com with a bit more detail about your setup, and we’ll take a closer look.

Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to help us make the game better.

reviews.review_title: Have the creators ever played tennis?
reviews.review_description:
The line calls in this game are ridiculous, yet when the bot hits a serve on the line it’s in but mine isn’t? I bought pro and it didn’t make my experience any better. Infact it made it worse, do 
not play.
i play tennis irl so of course i would enjoy this. sad part is it gets very repetitive and to get most fun things in the game you gotta pay real money. still is enjoyable with the 1v1 online mode.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a thoughtful review, MUS_IC, especially as someone who plays tennis in real life. We are glad to hear the core gameplay and online 1v1 are working for you.

One small clarification that may help. Match Point Tennis is designed so the full game is playable for free. All core modes, including single player, practice, and public and ranked multiplayer, are available without paying. Progression and gameplay are never gated behind purchases. Any paid content is strictly cosmetic or part of the optional PRO upgrade, which exists for players who want to further customize their experience or support ongoing development. There is no pay-to-win advantage.

We also hear the feedback around repetition. Ongoing updates, challenges, and seasonal content are a major focus for us, including free events like our recent Santa Challenge and 12 Days Before Christmas promotion.

We appreciate you playing and sharing your perspective, and we hope future updates give you even more reasons to jump back on court.

reviews.review_description: Great physics, great courts, and great bots! Not to mention ranked!

developer_response.body:
Really appreciate that positive feedback, MRGBURS. :) 
We've got more in store for January - hope you're enjoying the Santa Challenge and 12 Days Before Christmas event - more coming in January!

reviews.review_description:
This is not Wii Sports! 
This is a well thought out amazing game for all skill levels. But this is VR, you have to LEARN some techniques. ANYONE who slows down takes time to find their serve, figure out back hand just “HOW TO PLAY”. I promise you this is the best period!
(Little tip, hold your controller firm, not loose)

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much mr.ellrich! Glad you're enjoying the extra effort we put in on the physics system: we're glad you're finding your time investment rewarding! 

To others who are looking to quickly jump in and play right away - you can always dial in the physics difficulty settings to get exactly the kind of experience you're looking for. But as mr.ellrich said: if you spend some time in practice on your serve, returns, shots with the ball machine, and rallying and matches with the AI - you'll reap the benefits. :)

reviews.review_title: A Grand Slam of Graphics and Gameplay
reviews.review_description: I absolutely loved the game! The training sessions were incredible, the scenes were stunning, and the game physics felt as close to real tennis as it gets. 🎾

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, Pavan. Glad to hear the training modes, environments, and physics are landing well for you. We appreciate you taking the time to share it, and thanks for playing Match Point Tennis.

reviews.review_description:
Recommend novice and
Playing against npc for the best experience 
Easy to learn, don’t play tennis a lot but I’m good at the game

This a really great game, physics are awesome, multiplayer is smooth as butter, and is a lot of fun. Me and my friend had a absolute blast playing your game and made us feel like we were playing on a actual tennis court!
Fun to destroy my freind.

developer_response.body: Glad you’re having fun, pj_pancakes. Friendly rivalry is encouraged, friendships surviving it are a bonus. Thanks for playing and for the five stars. :D

reviews.review_description: warm - ups are easy but why is a solo quick match hard?

It feels like you are on the court! The creators have also done a good job with the room barriers, ensuring I’m not whacking my furniture around.

I’d like to support the developers and get the paid version, but I can’t find a way to upgrade to Pro.
